extraction.one_line_summary: KITTEL Marcel won stage 10 in dominant fashion, attacking from far back through the field with SABATINI Fabio's leadout to take his fourth stage victory.
tactical_patterns: SABATINI Fabio released KITTEL before final corner, allowing him to accelerate independently and prevent wheel-surfing counterattacks.
KITTEL deliberately delayed final acceleration to avoid giving rivals draft benefit, opening gap that couldn't be closed.
Team Sky positioned FROOME at front throughout flat stage instead of relying on protective positioning further back.
Teams stopped collaborating with QuickStep in chase because previous cooperation resulted in KITTEL dominance despite their leadout work.

rider_signals.KITTEL Marcel: positive: Won from implausibly far back with sustained power output that dropped CIMOLAI from his wheel mid-sprint.
rider_signals.SABATINI Fabio: positive: Demonstrated unusual composure releasing sprinter before final corner rather than traditional latest-possible handoff.
rider_signals.GREIPEL André: negative: Team worked on front throughout stage but finished 12th, indicating either tactical miscalculation or insufficient speed.
rider_signals.BOUHANNI Nacer: negative: Team contributed to chase work but unable to contest podium positions despite effort investment.
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: neutral: Team Sky executed defensive front-of-peloton positioning on supposedly routine sprint stage, suggesting heightened risk management after earlier crashes.
